name: Greensboro Housing Coalition
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit providing free housing counseling, emergency rental assistance, and healthy home services to low and moderate-income families in Greensboro, NC.
description_long:
Greensboro Housing Coalition (GHC) has operated as a community housing advocacy organization for over 36 years, establishing itself as a cornerstone resource for housing stability in the Greensboro area. The organization is built on the principle that everyone deserves fair, safe, healthy, and affordable housing, serving as an intermediary between struggling families and available community resources. GHC employs HUD-Certified Housing Counselors and maintains a dedicated housing hotline (336-691-9521) to provide immediate assistance to residents facing housing challenges.

GHC offers a comprehensive suite of free services organized around three operational pillars. Their Homeless and Rental Counseling services include personalized housing counseling, emergency shelter referrals, housing searches, and Emergency Tenant Assistance Program (ETAP) support. The Healthy Homes pillar conducts home assessments and inspections, provides rental lease reviews, facilitates landlord-tenant mediation, and distributes green cleaning kits. Community Engagement services encompass neighborhood activation, partnerships with government organizations, advocacy initiatives, and housing/non-housing resource referrals. Since 2020, GHC has received 145,000 calls, served 7,700 households, conducted 875 healthy home inspections, and helped save $16 million from mortgage foreclosures.

GHC distinguishes itself through measurable community impact and specialized programming. The organization administered over $5.7 million in COVID-era Emergency Rental Assistance Program (ERAP) funds, distributing 1,918 checks to 838 households during the pandemic. Their HUD certification provides credibility and ensures counseling meets federal standards. The organization maintains extended office hours (8am-5pm weekdays) and accepts walk-ins, reducing barriers to access. Their multi-faceted approach addresses not just housing crises but preventative healthy housing and community engagement.

GHC is genuinely a free non-profit service appropriate for low-income renters, families facing eviction, unhoused individuals, and those seeking affordable housing options. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—services are specific to the Greensboro, North Carolina area. While their track record is strong, they are community-dependent on donations and government grants, meaning some specialized services may have wait times or eligibility restrictions based on funding availability.
pros: HUD-Certified Housing Counselors providing free professional counseling services
24/7 Housing Hotline (336-691-9521) for real-time emergency assistance
Distributed $5.7M+ in emergency rental assistance during COVID-19 pandemic
Conducted 875 healthy home inspections since 2020 with free assessment services
Helped prevent $16 million in mortgage foreclosures over recent years
Three-pillar approach addressing counseling, healthy homes, and community engagement simultaneously
Extended office hours (8am-5pm, Monday-Friday) with in-person intake available
cons: Services limited to Greensboro, North Carolina area only
Dependent on government grants and donations—funding fluctuations may limit service availability
Website does not specify eligibility income thresholds or document requirements clearly
ETAP assistance appears limited to emergency situations and may have waitlists
No indication of multilingual staff or translation services availability
best_for: Renters facing eviction or lease disputes in Greensboro area
Low to moderate-income individuals seeking affordable housing guidance
Unhoused or chronically homeless persons needing shelter placement and support
Homeowners seeking free healthy home assessments and landlord-tenant mediation
diagnosis: GHC is best for Greensboro-area renters, families facing housing instability, and unhoused individuals seeking free HUD-certified counseling and emergency assistance. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to Greensboro, NC—residents outside this area will need to contact their local HUD-approved housing counselor or non-profit equivalent.
services: Housing Hotline triage and emergency assistance (336-691-9521)
Housing searches tailored to client preferences and affordability
Home assessments and healthy home inspections
Neighborhood engagement and community activation events
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
